compute.cost_note: GPU fine-tune for calibration (RoBERTa-large ~355M); CPU inference with a calibrated model.

do_not_use_when: You want an absolute "% AI-edited" number (this surface refuses it by design)
You have no same-corpus calibration (uncalibrated runs emit a score with no band)
You need cross-corpus comparability or per-sentence localization

artifacts: stdout markdown report (default), --json envelope, or --out PATH

purpose: Clean-room EditLens-style document-level edit-magnitude estimate — a RoBERTa-family regressor trained with MSE against a BERTScore-style similarity proxy between pre/post-edit pairs, framed as a SAME-CORPUS calibrated estimate with explicit OOD caveats, never an absolute "% AI". Uncalibrated by default (score only, no band); a calibrated model is operator-supplied via --model and applies only to the corpus it was calibrated on. The claim-license refuses an absolute %-AI / dosage claim, cross-corpus generalization, and per-sentence localization, and states the OOD-collapse caveat (degree estimation works in-distribution, collapses toward binary out-of-distribution).
use_when: You have an operator-calibrated model for a SPECIFIC corpus and want a same-corpus edit-magnitude estimate
You're assembling a multi-signal evidence pack and want a document-level magnitude reading
